title: Spring Quiet by Christina Rossetti — Nature Poetry Lines
content:
Gone were but the Winter,
Come were but the Spring,
I would go to a covert
Where the birds sing;

Where in the whitethorn
Singeth a thrush,
And a robin sings
meaning: "Spring Quiet" by Christina Rossetti is a nature poem consisting of 30 lines. The poet explores themes of nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Gone were but the Winter, Come were but the Spring,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Christina Rossetti's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
